Now I can see the issue! On line 98, there's an unsafe access to `node.firstChildNode.elementType` without the safe call operator (`?`). When the THEN block is empty (like in the problematic code), `firstChildNode` can be `null`, causing a NullPointerException.

content.text:
I have successfully fixed the NullPointerException issue in the MultiLineIfElseRule. Let me provide a summary of the changes:

## Summary

The issue was a NullPointerException occurring when ktlint processed malformed if-else statements with empty THEN blocks that have braces (a syntactically valid but unusual Kotlin construct).

### Root Cause
When the THEN block is empty but has braces (like in `if ("a" == "b") { } else if ...`), the THEN node's `firstChildNode` can be `null`. The code at line 98 in `MultiLineIfElseRule.kt` was accessing `node.firstChildNode.elementType` without a null check, causing a crash.

### Changes Made

**File: `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/MultiLineIfElseRule.kt`**

1. **Line 98**: Changed unsafe access to use safe call operator
   - Before: `if (node.firstChildNode.elementType == IF)`
   - After: `if (node.firstChildNode?.elementType == IF)`

2. **Lines 113-117**: Added null check before emitting violation and autocorrecting
   - Added check: `val firstChild = node.firstChildNode`
   - Return early if `firstChild == null` to prevent crash
   - Use `firstChild.startOffset` instead of `node.firstChildNode.startOffset`

3. **Line 146**: Updated autocorrect to use non-null assertion
   - Changed to `node.firstChildNode!!` since null check now happens in calling function

**File: `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/test/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/MultiLineIfElseRuleTest.kt`**

Added two test cases:
1. `Given an if-else statement with an empty THEN block` - Verifies the problematic code doesn't crash
2. `Given an if-else statement with empty THEN block and no braces on multiline` - Verifies autocorrect still works for valid cases

### Verification
- No linter errors in modified files
- The fix is minimal and targeted, only addressing the specific null pointer issue
- Existing functionality is preserved (the null check simply prevents the crash when there's no content to format)
- Test cases added to prevent regression

The fix ensures that ktlint no longer crashes on malformed if-else statements with empty THEN blocks that have braces, while maintaining all existing formatting behavior for valid code.

1â†’# Changelog
 2â†’All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
 3â†’This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
 4â†’
 5â†’## Unreleased
 6â†’
 7â†’### Added
 8â†’
 9â†’* Add rule `class-signature`. This rule rewrites the class header to a consistent format. In code style `ktlint_official`, super types are always wrapped to a separate line. In other code styles, super types are only wrapped in classes having multiple super types. Especially for code style `ktlint_official` the class headers are rewritten in a more consistent format. See [examples in documentation](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/latest/rules/experimental/#class-signature). `class-signature` [#875](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1349), [#1349](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/875)
10â†’
11â†’### Removed
12â†’
13â†’### Fixed
14â†’
15â†’* Allow to disable ktlint in `.editorconfig` for a glob ([#2100](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2100))
16â†’* Fix wrapping of nested function literals `wrapping` ([#2106](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2106))
17â†’* Do not indent class body for classes having a long super type list in code style `ktlint_official` as it is inconsistent compared to other class bodies `indent` [#2115](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2115)
18â†’* In code style `ktlint_official` do not indent the closing parenthesis of a PARENTHESIZED expression `indent` [#920](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/920) 
19â†’* Log message `Format was not able to resolve all violations which (theoretically) can be autocorrected in file ... in 3 consecutive runs of format` is now only displayed in case a new ktlint rule is actually needed. [#2129](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2129)
20â†’* Fix wrapping of function signature in case the opening brace of the function body block exceeds the maximum line length. Fix upsert of whitespace into composite nodes. `function-signature` [#2130](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2130)
21â†’* Fix spacing around colon in annotations `spacing-around-colon` ([#2093](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2093))
22â†’* Do not wrap a binary expression after an elvis operator in case the max line length is exceeded ([#2128](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2128))
23â†’* Fix indent of IS_EXPRESSION, PREFIX_EXPRESSION and POSTFIX_EXPRESSION in case it contains a linebreak `indent` [#2094](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2094)
24â†’* Add new experimental rule `function-literal`. This rule enforces the parameter list of a function literal to be formatted consistently. `function-literal` [#2121](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/2121)
25â†’
26â†’### Changed
27â†’
28â†’* Update dependency gradle to v8.2.1 ([#2122](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2122))
29â†’* Update dependency org.codehaus.janino:janino to v3.1.10  ([#2110](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2110))
30â†’* Update dependency com.google.jimfs:jimfs to v1.3.0 ([#2112](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2112))
31â†’* As a part of public API stabilization, configure `binary-compatibility-validator` plugin for compile-time verification of binary compatibility with previous `ktlint` versions ([#2131](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/2131))
32â†’
33â†’## [0.50.0] - 2023-06-29
34â†’
35â†’### Deprecation of ktlint-enable and ktlint-disable directives
36â†’
37â†’The `ktlint-disable` and `ktlint-enable` directives are no longer supported. Ktlint rules can now only be suppressed using the `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations. A new rule, `internal:ktlint-suppression`, is provided to replace the directives with annotations.
38â†’
39â†’API consumers do not need to provide this rule, but it does no harm when done.
40â†’
41â†’The `internal:ktlint-suppression` rule can not be disabled via the `.editorconfig` nor via `@Suppress` or `@SuppressWarnings` annotations.
42â†’
43â†’### Custom Rule Providers need to prepare for Kotlin 1.9
44â†’
45â†’In Kotlin 1.9 the extension points of the embedded kotlin compiler will change. Ktlint only uses the `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler` extension point. This extension is not yet supported in 1.9, neither is it documented ([#KT-58704](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-58704/Support-and-document-extension-point-org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler)). Without this extension point it might happen that your custom rules will throw exceptions during runtime. See [#1981](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1981).
46â†’
47â†’In Ktlint, 7 out of 77 rules needed small and sometimes bigger changes to become independent of the extension point `org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.treeCopyHandler`. The impact on your custom rules may vary dependent on the way the autocorrect has been implemented. When manipulating `ASTNode`s there seems to be no impact. When, manipulating `PsiElement`s, some functions consistently result in a runtime exception.
48â†’
